description: AI pipeline that generates 7 custom prospect assets after Call 1 to increase Call 2 show rates for Quantum Scaling reps

current_state.status: TAM 3-method rework shipped; lead-list sparse/degenerate handling added (fill 25 with email-less backfill at 0 credits + degenerate-query warning)

project_overview.what: Deal Forge is an internal sales tool for Quantum Scaling reps. After completing a Call 1 (Business Audit), the rep triggers the app with the Fireflies meeting ID. An AI pipeline extracts the prospect's ICP and business context, then generates 7 custom assets: a verified lead list, interactive Google Calendar invite visual, 3 webinar title options, an interactive ROI calculator pre-populated with their numbers, a prospect research profile, and brand-scraped visual identity. All assets are ready before Call 2. The in-portal Webinar Experience tab renders a one-slide branded hero + ICP-aware live chat at view time (no separate pipeline task).
project_overview.why: QS's Call 2 show rate is 37.5% — the core constraint on revenue. Prospects feel informed after Call 1 and have no strong pull to return. Deal Forge creates a concrete bridge: assets so specific to the prospect that skipping Call 2 means walking away from something already built for them. The 'sunk cost' and perceived value dramatically increase show rate and close rate.
project_overview.end_state: A polished internal rep dashboard used for every Call 2 booking. Rep selects today's calls from auto-loaded Fireflies meetings, clicks Generate, and has a full asset package ready within 20 minutes. The ROI model and reg page mock have shareable prospect-facing URLs. Eventually expands to full CRM integration and automated follow-up.
project_overview.current_summary: Phase 2 backend live on Render. 7-task pipeline deployed (webinar_mock task removed 2026-05-12 — standalone HTML asset was unused; in-portal Webinar Experience tab now renders a single brand-colors hero + ICP-aware chat client-side). Rep portal live at deal-forge-angel.onrender.com. Key systems confirmed working: (1) Fireflies transcript lookup — 6-pass search strategy (GHL name → company → email segments → domain → recent scan p1 → p2), prefers exact email match → domain match → loose keyword. (2) GHL contact lookup — enriches contactInfo with name/company/title before Fireflies search for better matching. (3) extractBriefFromTranscript — Claude Sonnet extracts full structured brief: prospect info, ICP (apollo_titles, apollo_industries, apollo_employee_ranges, apollo_geography, person_seniorities), metrics (LTV/close/show rate), angle (pain/result/methodology/proof), verbatim quotes, situation, context, webinar title seeds. (4) /api/prefetch endpoint — runs Fireflies + GHL + website scrape + Claude extraction before job creation so rep can review the brief. (5) Task handlers wired: extract, prospect_research, brand_scrape, lead_list, webinar_titles, roi_model, calendar_visual. Main blocker: lead_list EU Apollo coverage — org search returns 0 for Baltic/EU ICPs. Latest fix deployed (commit c934325): strip 'Europe' from geo (invalid Apollo value), EU country list retry with no AND-logic industry filter, geoUsed flag tells classifier to accept EU-wide companies. Unconfirmed — stopped testing to avoid rate limit loop.

resume_here: webinar_titles legacy 4-field schema removed 2026-05-14. Root cause of schema-drift regressions: loadCopyBrain rendered few-shot examples as Title+Description blob, training Claude to ignore the 12-field schema. Fix: (1) parseExampleDescription splits stored example blobs into 12 fields at prompt-build time so examples teach the new shape; (2) handleCalendarVisual composes description from the 9 narrative fields, drops `variant.description ‖ hook+bullets+for_line` fallback; (3) renderer drops `‖ v.hook` fallback; (4) spec §5 rewritten to canonical 12-field schema; (5) per-variant shape guard in generateWebinarTitles hard-fails on legacy/incomplete shape — no retry, surfaces regressions visibly. No DB migration. Test plan: trigger fresh webinar_titles on a job, confirm all 12 content fields + _score + _analysis present; re-run on 4c57375a-72c4-4fb3-be0e-5ab7d75cea54 manually to repair. Previous lead_list EU Apollo blocker still open (separate workstream).

Everything else confirmed working: Fireflies 6-pass transcript lookup, GHL contact enrichment, website scrape, Claude brief extraction, all 7 task handlers wired
immediate_next_steps: Fresh session: trigger one real job for rait@planpro.ee, watch Render logs — need to see which Apollo org search tier fires and what count it returns
If Apollo EU org search still 0: try people/search direct with person_locations (person-level geo, separate from org search) — may have better EU coverage

known_issues.title: lead_list returns 0 leads for Baltic/EU ICPs — Apollo org search returns 0 even with explicit EU country list + geo+size only filter
Apollo q_organization_keyword_tags uses AND logic — multiple industry tags returns 0 EU results. Workaround deployed: EU retry drops industry filter entirely.
In-process job worker — jobs lost on Render redeploy if in-flight. Jobs persist in Supabase but processing promise is killed.

next_phase.name: Phase 2 - Build & Test

Stage 2: brand_scrape, lead_list, webinar_titles, roi_model handlers (parallel)

recent_milestones.description: TAM decoupled from Apollo lead search + Webinar/Onboarding sub-tabs (from Lloyd↔Alex Call-1 review). (1) New Stage-2 `tam_estimate` task: Claude query-planner proposes 3-5 broad-but-valid Apollo slices, we probe each for real total_entries, headline TAM = largest deduped count × capped union_uplift (never summed/fabricated); keeps tam_floor (narrow count) for a 'verified in Apollo' subline. Smoke test (scripts/test-tam-estimate.js): floor 8,279 → TAM 55,389 (6.7x) on a sample SaaS ICP. Fixes Alex's '10-20x undercount → false disqualification'. Portal precedence: _overrides.tam_total > tam_estimate.output > _generated (Apollo) > lead_list.total, so a lead re-run never clobbers the estimate and jobs with no tam_estimate task fall back to today's Apollo number (no regression — verified on an old job showing 64,468 unchanged). (2) 3rd TAM field 'Full Market Cycle' now rep-editable (override full_market_cycle; cleared when TAM/outreach/ICP change). (3) computeRecommendedOutreach() centralizes the outreach formula that was duplicated in 3 places. (4) mockup-portal.html: generic switchSubTab() + Webinar Experience 3 sub-tabs (Live Preview / How We Build It / Continuous Optimization) + Onboarding 2nd sub-tab (What We Optimize lever map) — static visual talking aids. (5) DISABLE_WORKER=1 env flag for safe local testing against the shared Supabase. (6) Job-creation TAM-method toggle in mockup-dashboard.html ('AI Grounded' vs 'Apollo Exact') → POST /api/jobs tam_method → stamped on extracted_data.tam_method (survives extract's mergeBrief) → checkAndSpawnStageTasks spawns tam_estimate only when method='llm' (default); 'apollo' skips it so the portal shows Apollo's exact count with no estimated badge. TAM PLANNER FIX (after first local test showed a weak LLM number): root causes were (a) the LLM kept the narrow industry keyword on every slice — the single biggest under-counter (REACHUM: 1,378 with 'pharmaceuticals' vs 31,131 without) — and (b) it emitted human department phrases ('learning and development') that Apollo silently zeroes. Fixes: rewrote TAM_PLANNER_SYSTEM to force at least one no-industry-keyword broad slice + wide title unions; added VALID_DEPARTMENTS + normalizeDepartments() (maps L&D/training/talent→human_resources etc.); added an unbounded-slice guard (rejects seniority-only slices that match millions). Verified on the real REACHUM ICP: 1,378 → 88,927 (64.5×), fully grounded in real Apollo counts. REFINEMENT (Gary's steer): rather than dropping the industry entirely, the planner now KEEPS the industry but broadens it to ADJACENT verticals fetched from Apollo's real taxonomy via suggestIndustries() (pharmaceuticals -> +biotechnology, hospital & health care, medical devices, research) — the 'LLM picks from real Apollo options' path (free, no credits). Industry values snapped server-side to the canonical adjacent set (floor=seed only; broad=adjacent set, backfilled, never dropped); broaden-the-role set to function-broad per Gary. STABILITY FIXES (number was swinging 995/4K/207K between runs): (1) adjacency was using suggestIndustries() which query-matches and surfaced narrow '*pharmaceuticals' sub-types (~1K) — replaced with fetchAdjacentIndustries() ranking the primary canonical o.industry by company frequency, dropping seed-near-synonyms (per_page:100); (2) aggregation changed from MAX slice (caught explosive outliers → 207K) to MEDIAN of broad slices, dropping duds below 20% of the top broad slice. Now stable ~25-35K on REACHUM across runs (median lands in the role-faithful band; function-broad-via-max was the unstable extreme). Added Prospect Infos TAM-method display + toggle + '↻ Rerun TAM' (POST /api/jobs/:id/rerun-tam runs inline; 'apollo' skips the task → Apollo exact). THIRD TAM METHOD 'llm_pure' (label 'AI Estimate') added per Gary: Claude estimates TAM from its own knowledge with NO Apollo probing (estimateTamPure(), temp 0 → stable; prompt broadens across adjacent verticals + role variants), badged 'Estimated', tam_source 'llm_pure_estimate', tam_floor null. Apollo still fetches the 25 leads. Three-way toggle now on BOTH the job-creation modal (mockup-dashboard.html) and the Prospect Infos tab (mockup-portal.html): AI Grounded / AI Estimate / Apollo Exact. DEFAULT at job creation is now Apollo Exact (rep opts into an AI method); dashboard toggle default + POST /api/jobs fallback + Prospect Infos display default all 'apollo'. rerun-tam + POST /api/jobs + checkAndSpawnStageTasks all handle the 3 values (llm & llm_pure spawn/run tam_estimate; apollo skips). REACHUM: Apollo ~991, AI Estimate ~18.5K (stable), AI Grounded ~25-35K. WEBINAR/ONBOARDING VISUALS polished (per rep feedback): 'How We Build It' rebuilt as a larger AI+Expert engine → 5-step arrow timeline; 'Continuous Optimization' rebuilt as a conversion-rate-compounding bar chart (3%→30% over 5 runs) + a representative full-velocity funnel; Onboarding lever map enlarged with numbered cards. All static, brand-aware, div-balanced. Additive migration 20260714 widens tasks_task_type_check to include tam_estimate (applied + verified). Spec: specs/tam_estimate.md. Local-tested (worker-disabled); NOT yet committed/pushed — awaiting rep sign-off.

webinar_titles legacy 4-field schema removed — old {title, hook, description, for_line} shape was dead weight in the codebase (5 fallback sites across server.js, mockup-portal.html, spec) but the few-shot examples_block kept rendering example bodies as Title+Description blobs, training Claude back into the old shape via demonstrations dominating over instructions. Fix: parseExampleDescription splits stored example blobs into the 12-field shape at prompt-build time so demonstrations now match the schema; handleCalendarVisual composes description from the 9 narrative fields; renderer fallback removed; spec §5 rewritten to canonical 12-field schema; per-variant shape guard hard-fails on legacy shape so future regressions surface immediately in tasks.error_message.
Problem validated: Call 2 show rate 37.5% confirmed as core revenue constraint
Pipeline architecture designed and approved (batch-design + state-machine) — originally 8 tasks; webinar_mock removed 2026-05-12 (current count: 7)
Cost check passed: ~$0.05-$0.08/job, ~$5-8/month at scale
Schema designed, reviewed, and applied to Supabase (sales_assets schema, jobs + tasks tables)
Supabase Storage bucket sales-assets created
Fireflies API isolated and validated — Notes field confirmed as primary extraction source
/task-spec skill created — mandatory 11-section gate before any task code is written
extract task fully specced (all 11 sections)
webinar_titles task fully specced (all 11 sections, 300-word limit, needs_input status, format brain architecture)
24 QS case studies scraped from due-diligence site and written to Lloyd OS Brain — Consulting x7, Software x5, Coaching x7, Agencies x5
prospect_research task specced (LinkedIn-only, Claude bio synthesis, jobs.research_data output)
brand_scrape task specced (Apify web-scraper, color extraction priority order, jobs.brand_data output)
lead_list task specced — 5-step pipeline with deterministic website quality gate, Apollo.io API (replaced Ample Leads), company_size in output
roi_model task specced — no LLM call, pure template-fill + math, interactive HTML, rep edits live on Call 2, shareable prospect URL
calendar_visual task specced — Google Calendar popup mock, webinar copy injected from prior tasks, Haiku generates 3 reminder emails, Yes click reveals email sequence
Phase 1 spec gate complete — all pipeline tasks fully specced. Ready for /ux-design and Phase 2 build.
Phase 2 backend built and deployed to Render — pipeline live, GHL webhook, Fireflies lookup, rep portal at deal-forge-angel.onrender.com

webinar_mock task removed — standalone HTML asset deprecated (no one opened it); in-portal Webinar Experience tab simplified to a single brand-colors hero slide + ICP-aware chat panel. Pipeline drops from 8 → 7 tasks.
Fireflies 6-pass transcript lookup confirmed working: GHL name → company → email segments → domain → recent scan (p1+p2). Prefers exact email attendee → domain attendee → loose keyword match.

extractBriefFromTranscript confirmed working: Claude Sonnet extracts full structured brief with 8 top-level sections — prospect, icp (incl. apollo_titles, apollo_industries, apollo_employee_ranges, apollo_geography, person_seniorities), metrics, angle, verbatim, situation, context, titles.
/api/prefetch endpoint confirmed working: GHL lookup + Fireflies + website scrape + Claude extraction in one call. Rep reviews brief before confirming job.
lead_list pipeline hardened: no-name filter, enterprise employee ranges, anyMatch outage detection (vs genuine rejection), geo cleanup (strip 'European Union'), classifier geo hard-reject rule

Apollo AND-logic bug identified: q_organization_keyword_tags with 5 industry values returns 0 EU results. EU retry now uses geo+size only (no industry filter), geoUsed flag tells classifier to accept EU-wide companies.

decision_log.decision: Separate Supabase project (not shared with QS Dashboard)
decision_log.reasoning: Every existing app has its own Supabase project. Sharing would mix concerns and compete for connection limits.

decision_log.decision: Hard delete on jobs (no soft delete)
decision_log.reasoning: Job records are operational queue state, not compliance records. Assets persist in Supabase Storage independently.

decision_log.decision: ROI model built as interactive HTML page (not spreadsheet)
decision_log.reasoning: Opens in browser alongside other assets. Pre-populated with extracted LTV. Progressive disclosure rep view + shareable prospect view (?view=prospect).

decision_log.decision: lead_list uses 4-step sub-pipeline (ICP extraction → lead fetch → website crawl → ICP match filter)
decision_log.reasoning: Raw Apify/Ample Leads results are low quality (~50% irrelevant). Website crawl + Claude ICP classification filters to 25 verified matches.
All Stage 2 tasks need this data. Writing to the job record makes it accessible without joins through the tasks table.

decision_log.decision: Webinar slides cut from asset list
decision_log.reasoning: Gamma has no API. Slides require full narrative depth impossible from a 30-min call.

decision_log.decision: lead_list uses Apollo.io People Search API directly — Ample Leads (Apify) dropped
decision_log.reasoning: Dev team already has Apollo credits. Direct API is cleaner than Apify actor wrapper — better documented, no actor ID uncertainty, no additional Apify cost layer.

decision_log.decision: prospect_research = LinkedIn only. brand_scrape = website only.
decision_log.reasoning: Original spec had prospect_research scraping both sources, creating overlap with brand_scrape. Clean separation: one source per task, no redundant HTTP calls.

decision_log.decision: reg_page replaced by webinar_mock (live webinar experience simulator)
decision_log.reasoning: Calendar invite IS the registration mechanism — showing a reg page alongside it creates cognitive dissonance ('just click Yes' vs 'also fill out this form'). webinar_mock is more powerful: shows the prospect their brand applied to the full webinar experience (slides + live chat), creating the 'oh shit' moment. Same brand data is used, better outcome.

The standalone HTML asset (generated server-side, stored in Supabase, opened via a 'View personalized webinar' button) saw no rep usage. The same hero + chat experience renders client-side inside the portal's Webinar Experience tab — no separate asset needed. Pipeline simplifies to 7 tasks. Hero slide also flipped to brand-colors-only by default (image is opt-in via the asset picker) since reps preferred the cleaner look.
